title: Area of parallelograms
description: Practice finding the area of parallelograms given base and height.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the image below. 

[[☃ image 2]]

**What is the area of the parallelogram?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] square units
hints:
Let's imagine we cut the parallelogram on the dotted line. We could slide part of the shape to make a rectangle.

Then we could find the area of the rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of the parallelogram is the same as the area of a $7$-unit by $5$-unit rectangle.
$\text{Area of parallelogram} = \text{Area of rectangle} = \text{base} \times \text{height}$
$\text{Area of parallelogram}  = 7 \times 5=35$ square units

options.alt: A parallelogram. The segment representing the height of the parallelogram is in the middle of the figure, splitting the figure in 2 parts. The base of the parallelogram is 7 units and the height of the parallelogram is 5 units. The diagonal side length is 6 units.

assessment_items.question_content:

hints:

Then we could find the area of the rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of the parallelogram is the same as the area of a $6$-unit by $4$-unit rectangle.
$\text{Area of parallelogram} = \text{Area of rectangle} = \text{base} \times \text{height}$
$\text{Area of parallelogram}  = 6 \times 4=24$ square units

options.alt: A parallelogram. The segment representing the height of the parallelogram is in the middle of the figure, splitting the figure in 2 parts. The base of the parallelogram is 6 units and the height of the parallelogram is 4 units. The diagonal side length is 5 units.

**Find the area of the parallelogram.**

hints:
Let's imagine we cut the parallelogram on the dotted line. We could slide part of the shape to make a rectangle.

Then we could find the area of the rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of the parallelogram is the same as the area of a $7$-unit by $5$-unit rectangle.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area of parallelogram} &= \text{Area of rectangle}\\\\
&= \text{base} \times \text{height}\\\\
&= 7 \times 5
\end{align}$
The area of the parallelogram is $35$ square units
